prevalence.source: GATS India and national tobacco surveillance
prevalence.trend: slowly decreasing overall; smokeless tobacco remains high

incidence.source: School health surveys and tobacco surveillance

burden.dalyPerCapita: high; major driver of CVD, COPD and cancers

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Self-reported tobacco use; assess dependence with standardized tools

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of millions of current or former users
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; cessation support varies by state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care screening and brief intervention
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Behavioral counseling, nicotine replacement, bupropion/varenicline where available; quitline support
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High smokeless tobacco use, limited cessation services, and relapse
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National tobacco control program and increasing smoke-free policies
